title: Configuration to reduce process clutter using podman remote

comments.body: @mheon ptal, you wrote the original code.

The basic logic is this: information about the exec session (e.g. its exit code) can only accessed via API after the exec session has already exited, so we must retain this information in the database for some period of time; Docker runs a timed sweep every 5 minutes to clear old exec sessions, which we can't easily do as we don't have a daemon, so we delay the exec session cleanup for 5 minutes to mimic this. I have no objection to making this configurable, as 5 minutes does seem to be drastically excessive for most users. Adding a `containers.conf` knob would seem appropriate.
Hi there, this seems like a pretty good first issue to pick up, could we (student team) try to tackle this?

Sure, feel free.

Modifications to containers.conf must begin in https://github.com/containers/common/tree/main/pkg/config - once that's done, wiring the change into the compat API endpoint shouldn't be that bad.

Thanks, so I started there and created the configuration knob. Now I face the next step changing exec to use the config like this
```
libpodConfig.ExitCommandDelay = runtimeConfig.Engine.ExitCommandDelay
```
but this gives me an error in make saying ExitCommandDelay is undefined. I tried to locally make common which is in the same rootdirectory as podman, but I'm guessing I'm missing some linking step. Do you perhaps have any suggestions?

You'll need to update the vendor of c/common - once the change is committed to the containers/common repo, you can use `go get github.com/containers/common@<COMMIT HASH>` and then `make vendor-in-container` to finalize the vendoring and get the updated c/common into Podman.

Thanks for the clarification. The common updates seem to have merged already now and it now builds using the configuration, however I can't reproduce the behaviour of the changed value yet. I have added the configuration into `containers.conf` file in `/usr/share/containers` and in `$HOME/.config/containers` for both the client and server but that does not seem to work. Does the locally build version use another file perhaps?
They should all use the same config files. I would recommend adding some debug prints to the c/common code in your local copy to see if the variable in the config file is being read in properly.
Alright thanks, I will investigate more then
I managed to get it working now, don't really know what was going wrong, but when I debugged it it suddenly worked! Anyway PR is open and ready to close this issue.
